column2: Fermentation Jar with Airlock — 4 Litre — Wide Mouth
column20: Family-size wide-mouth glass jar with airlock-ready lid. The hero of bigger ferments.
column25: Most jars in your kitchen weren't designed for fermentation. This one is. Wide mouth fits chunks. Smooth interior makes cleaning easy. Lid threads accept airlocks. Glass holds shape over years of use.

column20: Lets CO2 out. Keeps oxygen out. The single most important fermentation accessory.
column25: Without an airlock, your jar builds CO2 pressure and you have to "burp" it daily. With an airlock, the CO2 escapes through a water-sealed valve — and oxygen (which causes mould) can't get back in.
column26: [["CO2 OUT", "Fermentation gas escapes through water seal · no pressure buildup."], ["OXYGEN BLOCKED", "No oxygen in = no mould risk · safer ferments."], ["NO BURPING", "Truly hands-off · just check water level weekly."], ["REUSABLE", "Lifetime use with proper cleaning."]]

column20: Pushes vegetables under brine. Above brine = mould risk. Glass weight = safe ferment.
column25: During fermentation, vegetables float to the top. Anything above the brine line is exposed to air = mould. The glass weight pushes vegetables under, keeping every bite submerged and safe.
column26: [["SUBMERGED", "Pushes veggies fully under brine."], ["PURE GLASS", "No leaching · no rust · food-safe."], ["NOTCHED", "Easy to lift out without contamination."], ["SET OF 2", "One per jar · spare for second batch."]]
